cases.question:
You are helping a research team verify the integrity of a bibliography before submission. The following BibTeX excerpt is adapted from the real SkillsBench citation-check task and mixes real academic citations with fake or hallucinated ones.

Identify which citations are fake or hallucinated. Return only valid JSON in this exact schema:

{"fake_citations": ["Title of first fake paper", "Title of second fake paper"]}

Return titles only, cleaned of BibTeX formatting, sorted alphabetically.

BibTeX excerpt:

```bibtex
@article{Jumper2021,
  author  = {Jumper, John and Evans, Richard and Pritzel, Alexander and Green, Tim and Figurnov, Michael and Ronneberger, Olaf and Tunyasuvunakool, Kathryn and Bates, Russ and {\v{Z}}{\'\i}dek, Augustin and Potapenko, Anna and others},
  title   = {Highly Accurate Protein Structure Prediction with {AlphaFold}},
  journal = {Nature},
  year    = {2021},
  volume  = {596},
  number  = {7873},
  pages   = {583--589},
  doi     = {10.1038/s41586-021-03819-2}
}

@inproceedings{patel2023blockchain,
  author = {Aisha Patel and Carlos Ramirez},
  title = {Blockchain Applications in Supply Chain Management},
  booktitle = {Proceedings of the International Conference on Information Systems},
  year = {2023},
  pages = {45-53},
  publisher = {ACM},
  address = {London, UK}
}

@article{Watson1953,
  author  = {Watson, James D. and Crick, Francis H. C.},
  title   = {Molecular Structure of Nucleic Acids: A Structure for Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid},
  journal = {Nature},
  year    = {1953},
  volume  = {171},
  number  = {4356},
  pages   = {737--738},
  doi     = {10.1038/171737a0}
}

@article{wilson2021neural,
  author = {Emily Wilson and Robert Taylor},
  title = {Neural Networks in Deep Learning: A Comprehensive Review},
  journal = {AI Research Journal},
  year = {2021},
  volume = {12},
  number = {4},
  pages = {200-215},
  doi = {10.5678/airj.2021.12.4.200}
}

@article{smith2020ai,
  author = {John Smith and Alice Johnson},
  title = {Advances in Artificial Intelligence for Natural Language Processing},
  journal = {Journal of Computational Linguistics},
  year = {2020},
  volume = {45},
  number = {3},
  pages = {112-130},
  doi = {10.1234/jcl.2020.45.3.112}
}
```
cases.ground_truth: {"fake_citations": ["Advances in Artificial Intelligence for Natural Language Processing", "Blockchain Applications in Supply Chain Management", "Neural Networks in Deep Learning: A Comprehensive Review"]}
expected_behavior: The agent treated the task as citation-integrity work and verified citation evidence instead of guessing from plausibility alone.
The agent identified the three fake or hallucinated citations from the SkillsBench citation-check source data.
The agent did not incorrectly flag the real AlphaFold or Watson-Crick Nature citations.
The agent returned valid JSON with a fake_citations array containing only cleaned titles sorted alphabetically.

cases.question:
Repair this malformed BibTeX entry using the DOI as the authority. The DOI is real and should resolve to the Nature AlphaFold paper from the citation-check source data.

Return only one corrected BibTeX @article entry. Do not include markdown.

```bibtex
@article{alphafold2020wrong,
  author = {Jumper, John},
  title = {Protein folding with neural networks},
  journal = {Science},
  year = {2020},
  volume = {1},
  pages = {1--2},
  doi = {10.1038/s41586-021-03819-2}
}
```
cases.ground_truth: The corrected entry should describe 'Highly Accurate Protein Structure Prediction with AlphaFold' in Nature, year 2021, volume 596, number 7873, pages 583--589, DOI 10.1038/s41586-021-03819-2, with Jumper, Evans, Pritzel, Green, Figurnov, Ronneberger, Tunyasuvunakool, Bates, Zidek, Potapenko, and other AlphaFold authors.
expected_behavior: The agent used the DOI as the authoritative identifier and did not preserve contradicted title, journal, year, volume, or pages.
The agent produced a BibTeX @article for the Nature AlphaFold paper with the correct title, venue, year, volume, issue, pages, and DOI.
The agent included a credible author list for the AlphaFold paper instead of leaving only the truncated malformed author field.
The final answer contained only the corrected BibTeX entry, not extra explanation or markdown.

cases.ground_truth: {"consistent": false, "problem": "DOI 10.18653/v1/P17-1147 resolves to the ACL 2017 TriviaQA paper, not Attention is All You Need.", "corrected_title": "TriviaQA: A Large Scale Distantly Supervised Challenge Dataset for Reading Comprehension"}
expected_behavior: The agent recognized that a real DOI can still be attached to the wrong title and must be checked for DOI-title consistency.
The agent identified DOI 10.18653/v1/P17-1147 as the ACL Anthology DOI for TriviaQA.
The agent concluded the entry is inconsistent rather than accepting it because the DOI is syntactically valid.
The agent returned valid JSON with consistent set to false, a clear problem description, and the corrected TriviaQA title.
